title: Judges, police must keep their personal biases aside when dealing with rape cases: Retired Justice Gita Mittal
description: Former Chief Justice of the Jammu and Kashmir High Court Gita Mittal recently commented on the challenges faced by rape survivors both within and outside the courtroom. She noted that even legally trained minds including police and the courts may bring with them their own social baggage, which makes them reluctant to pro-actively tackle rape cases. In this regard, she recounted a case concerning the rape of an eight-year-old girl. The trial court judge had vaguely recorded the rape as “galat kaa
